title: crepis bakeri subsp. cusickii
intro: Crepis bakeri subsp. cusickii, also known as Cusick's hawk's beard or Cusick's hawkbit, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Crepis bakeri subsp. cusickii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to support overall health and well-being.
